Observed that the person in charge was unable to demonstrate knowledge of foodborne disease prevention and other requirements of the Food Code and local ordinances. Knowledge Tested: Observed PIC was not knowledgeable or responsible due to multiple priority violations such as no sanitizer available while in operations, improper cold holding, improper thawing, improper cooling, lack of hand washing, not aware of six reportable food borne illnesses, not aware of proper wash rinse and sanitize of food contact surfaces, was not aware of proper parts per million of chlorine sanitizer to properly clean and sanitize resulting in a closure of the facility. Instructed the PIC that all violations are the responsibility of the PIC and that they should review their responsibilities as outlined in Chapter 2 of the FDA food code and ensure that employees are trained in food safety. Send Sick Employees Home Now= Salmonella Typhi, Shigella, E. coli, Hepatitis A, Norovirus, Nontyphoidal Salmonella
